title: Transmission Electron Microscopy Protocol for Anaerobic Ciliates
author: Johana Rotterova
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.85uhy6w

steps.title: Isolate 1 ml of thriving culture...
steps.instruction: Isolate 1 ml of thriving culture of ciliate cells, fix with 2.5% (v/v) glutaraldehyde (Polysciences) and subsequently centrifuge at 800×g for 5 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Carefully replace supernatant with 1 ml of 2
steps.instruction: Carefully replace supernatant with 1 ml of 2.5% (v/v) glutaraldehyde in 0.2M SCB (Sodium Cacodylate buffer, pH 7.2) and incubate 1 hour on ice.

steps.title: Then, rinse the pellet carefully with...
steps.instruction: Then, rinse the pellet carefully with SCB three times per 15 minutes.

steps.title: Postfix the cells with 1% OsO4...
steps.instruction: Postfix the cells with 1% OsO4 in distilled water for 1 hour on ice.

steps.title: Rinse the cells with distilled water...
steps.instruction: Rinse the cells with distilled water (5 minutes), dehydrate them in a graded ethanol series from 30% to absolute ethanol: through 30%, 50%, 70%, 80%, 90%, 95%, and finally three times in absolute ethanol, each per eight minutes.

steps.title: Let the embedded cells get polymerized...
steps.instruction: Let the embedded cells get polymerized at 70 °C for 48 hours.

steps.title: Cut serial ultrathin sections on an...
steps.instruction: Cut serial ultrathin sections on an Ultracut E ultramicrotome (Reichert) using a diamond knife and stain with lead citrate and uranyl acetate (2–3%). Protocol is adapted from Rotterova et al. (2018) and Beinart et al. (2018). Tropidoatractidae fam. nov., a Deep Branching Lineage of Metopida (Armophorea, Ciliophora) Found in Diverse Habitats and Possessing Prokaryotic Symbionts.. Protist. The genome of an endosymbiotic methanogen is very similar to those of its free-living relatives.. Environmental microbiology.

steps.title: Incubate cells two times in 1:1...
steps.instruction: Incubate cells two times in 1:1 ethanol-acetone mixture per 15 minutes.

steps.title: Incubate cells once in 2 ml...
steps.instruction: Incubate cells once in 2 ml of 100% acetone per 15 minutes, and finally substitute aceton with acetone-resin mixture (EPON resin Poly/Bed 812, Polysciences) and incubate per 6-12 hours.

steps.title: Substitute absolute EPON resin (Poly/Bed 812,...
steps.instruction: Substitute absolute EPON resin (Poly/Bed 812, Polysciences) three times and finally embed cells in in the resin into capsules.
